skill.name: web-artifacts-builder
skill.description: Creates multi-component Claude HTML artifacts by scaffolding React, TypeScript, Vite, Tailwind CSS, and shadcn/ui projects, then bundling them into single-file artifacts.
skill.summary: Scaffold and bundle React-based Claude artifacts with Tailwind CSS and shadcn/ui.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis produced many high-risk hits, but the license and Tailwind color findings are false positives and no prompt injection or malicious exfiltration was found. The skill still carries medium risk because it instructs users to run shell scripts that install npm packages, modify project files, remove build outputs, and extract a bundled component archive.

medium_findings.title: Shell Scripts Install and Execute External Package Tooling
medium_findings.description: The initialization and bundling scripts run pnpm, npm, Vite, Parcel, and html-inline commands. This is expected for a frontend artifact builder, but it executes third-party package tooling and package lifecycle scripts from the user environment.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The scripts plainly invoke package managers and build tools. The behavior is legitimate for the skill, but the supply-chain execution risk is real.

medium_findings.title: Project Filesystem Mutation and Build Cleanup
medium_findings.description: The scripts create and overwrite configuration files, edit TypeScript configuration, extract component files, and remove prior build output. These operations are scoped to the generated project, but users should run them only in intended project directories.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The filesystem writes and cleanup commands are explicit. Risk is moderate because paths are mostly project-local and there is no evidence of arbitrary file access.

medium_findings.title: Bundled Archive Extracted Into Generated Project
medium_findings.description: The initialization script extracts shadcn component files from a local archive into the project source directory. The archive supports the skill purpose, but archive extraction is a supply-chain and path-safety concern for marketplace review.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The archive extraction is directly visible in the script. No malicious archive behavior is shown in the audited text, so the concern remains moderate.

low_findings.title: False Positive Weak Cryptography Matches
low_findings.description: The reported weak cryptography matches occur in Apache License legal text and Tailwind CSS color variable names such as destructive. I found no evidence of MD5, SHA-1, DES, or other weak cryptographic use.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line context shows license prose and CSS theme tokens, not cryptographic algorithms or security-sensitive hashing.

low_findings.title: False Positive Shell Backtick Matches in Documentation
low_findings.description: The SKILL.md matches are Markdown inline code blocks and fenced shell examples that instruct users how to run the included scripts. They are not hidden execution by the skill file itself.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The syntax is Markdown formatting around commands and filenames. The operational risk is covered by the script findings, not by documentation parsing.

low_findings.title: Benign Hardcoded Documentation URLs
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URLs point to the Apache License and shadcn/ui documentation. I found no evidence of data exfiltration, tracking endpoints, or credential transmission.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are static documentation or schema references. They are not used in network request code that sends user data.

low_findings.title: No Prompt Injection Attempt Found
low_findings.description: The skill includes style guidance and optional testing guidance, but I found no text that claims system authority, asks the evaluator to skip analysis, or attempts to override marketplace review instructions.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed guidance is task-related and does not contain override language or special-authority claims.

low_findings.title: False Positive Dynamic Import Match
low_findings.description: The reported dynamic import expression is a Tailwind JSDoc type annotation inside generated configuration text. It is not runtime module loading or user-controlled dynamic code execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is inside a quoted heredoc for tailwind.config.js and only documents a config type. It does not execute import() at runtime.

low_findings.title: False Positive Reconnaissance Matches
low_findings.description: The reported reconnaissance lines are style and optional testing guidance in SKILL.md. They do not collect host information, enumerate files, or instruct secret discovery.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The context is user-facing artifact guidance. There is no command or procedure for system enumeration on these lines.

low_findings.title: Benign Tool Detection and Script Directory Resolution
low_findings.description: Some shell command substitution findings detect Node version, current script location, and bundle size. These are ordinary setup checks and do not include user-controlled command construction.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The substitutions call fixed utilities and assign fixed setup metadata. The project name is quoted where it is used in command arguments.

dangerous_patterns.title: Package Manager Execution
dangerous_patterns.description: The scripts run npm and pnpm commands that can download packages and execute package lifecycle hooks.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are visible and expected. The risk is from third-party package execution, not from hidden malicious code.

dangerous_patterns.title: Destructive Build Cleanup Command
dangerous_patterns.description: The bundling script removes dist and bundle.html before rebuilding. This is scoped to the current directory but can delete user files with those names if run in the wrong directory.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The rm command is explicit. It is a common build cleanup operation, but its effect depends on the current working directory.

dangerous_patterns.title: Archive Extraction Into Source Directory
dangerous_patterns.description: The initialization script extracts a local tar.gz archive into src. Marketplace review should treat embedded archives as higher-trust assets that need provenance control.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The tar extraction command is explicit. The text does not show path traversal, but archive extraction is inherently sensitive.

content.value_statement: Complex Claude artifacts often need React state, reusable UI, and a reliable bundling path. This skill scaffolds a Vite project with Tailwind CSS and shadcn/ui, then bundles it into one shareable HTML artifact.
actual_capabilities: Initializes a React 18 and TypeScript project with Vite.
Configures Tailwind CSS 3.4.1 with shadcn/ui theme variables.
Adds path aliases for imports from the src directory.
Installs Radix UI, shadcn/ui dependencies, and related UI libraries.
Extracts prepackaged shadcn/ui components into the project.
Bundles a project into one self-contained HTML file using Parcel and html-inline.
limitations: Requires Node.js 18 or newer and a working npm or pnpm environment.
Runs package manager commands that need network access unless packages are cached.
Targets complex React artifacts, not small single-file HTML snippets.
Does not automatically verify the visual quality or behavior of the generated artifact.

use_cases.title: Prototype Interactive Dashboards
use_cases.description: Create a React artifact with stateful controls, charts, and reusable UI components for stakeholder review.

use_cases.title: Package Rich Claude Demos
use_cases.description: Build a multi-file frontend demo and convert it into one HTML file that can be shared as a Claude artifact.

use_cases.title: Standardize Component-Based Artifacts
use_cases.description: Start from a repeatable Tailwind CSS and shadcn/ui setup instead of rebuilding project configuration each time.

prompt_templates.title: Create a New Artifact Project
prompt_templates.prompt: Use web-artifacts-builder to initialize a new project named customer-dashboard. Keep the default React, Tailwind CSS, and shadcn/ui setup.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Stateful Interface
prompt_templates.prompt: Use web-artifacts-builder to create a React artifact for comparing three pricing plans. Include tabs, form controls, and responsive layout.

prompt_templates.title: Bundle for Sharing
prompt_templates.prompt: Use web-artifacts-builder to bundle the current React artifact into a single HTML file that can be shared in a Claude conversation.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Complete Demo Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Use web-artifacts-builder to scaffold, implement, and bundle a polished analytics demo with shadcn/ui components and local state management.

output_examples.input: Build a product comparison artifact with filters and cards.
output_examples.output: A Vite React project configured with Tailwind CSS, shadcn/ui components, and a bundled HTML artifact ready to share.

output_examples.input: Package my existing artifact project for Claude.
output_examples.output: A single self-contained HTML file with application code, styles, and assets inlined for artifact delivery.

output_examples.input: Create an internal tool prototype with dialogs and tables.
output_examples.output: A component-based frontend prototype using preinstalled UI primitives and project-local configuration files.

best_practices: Run the scripts inside a new or disposable project directory.
Review package installation output before sharing the final artifact.
Test the bundled HTML in a browser when the artifact has complex interactions.
anti_patterns: Do not run the bundling script from a directory that contains unrelated dist or bundle.html files.
Do not use this skill for simple static HTML that does not need React or build tooling.
Do not assume generated UI is production-ready without accessibility and behavior review.

faq.question: What does this skill create?
faq.answer: It creates a React, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, and shadcn/ui project for complex Claude HTML artifacts.

faq.question: Does it produce a single HTML file?
faq.answer: Yes. The bundle script builds the project and inlines assets into bundle.html.

faq.question: Does it need network access?
faq.answer: Usually yes. The scripts install npm packages unless the required packages are already cached.

faq.question: Is this suitable for small snippets?
faq.answer: No. It is designed for multi-component artifacts that need a frontend build process.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: What should users review before running it?
faq.answer: Users should review the shell scripts, package changes, and current working directory before execution.
